evidence.observation: Primary editorial headings are the exact business name or two-word phrases: Useful Directions, Local Context, and Confirm Details; body copy explains and Explore Ideas directs.
The same disciplined heading and body/action roles remain intact without extra headline clauses.
Headings wrap but retain their concise wording and distinct roles.

headlineDiscipline.fix: No required correction; preserve the concise heading system.

evidence.observation: The Planning Notes opening distributes its eyebrow and image at left while Useful Directions sits separately to the right; Local Context similarly separates the large “C” from its heading block.
The context emblem and textual heading remain separate columns rather than reading as one opening unit.
Local Context opens with a large isolated “C,” while the label and heading appear substantially farther down the section.

foldComposition.fix: Group each section’s emblem or image, eyebrow, heading, and introductory copy into one compact opening composition before the section detail.

evidence.observation: All three numbered guidance rows are present with a title and description; no empty columns or orphan cards are visible.
The repeating list remains complete as three evenly separated rows.
All three items stack completely with matching numbers, titles, and descriptions.

completeLayouts.fix: Preserve the complete three-row list structure.

evidence.observation: Rendered mechanics report 4 of 4 exact first beats visible in normal, reduced-motion, and no-JavaScript modes.
Rendered mechanics report 4 of 4 exact first beats visible in all three modes.
Rendered mechanics report 4 of 4 exact first beats visible in all three modes, despite the weak visual ordering of the context opening.

firstBeatVisibility.fix: Preserve unconditional rendering while improving the visual grouping of the context first beat.

evidence.observation: Hero and guidance text sits on solid cream, while Local Context text sits on solid charcoal rather than across the photographs.
Text remains separated from detailed image regions and has strong cream/charcoal contrast.
No essential copy overlays the busy wheel or street photographs; dark-section copy remains readable on a solid background.

imageContrast.fix: Preserve the solid text fields and avoid moving copy over the detailed workshop imagery.

evidence.observation: Only staged-hero-entrance is declared; normal mode has one visible target, reduced motion disables it at 0.01ms, and no-JavaScript retains visible content.
One restrained hero target is present; reduced-motion and no-JavaScript fallbacks pass with all first beats visible.
The same single movement contract passes, reduced motion disables it, and no content depends on JavaScript.

motionRestraint.fix: Keep motion limited to the existing hero entrance and do not animate the large decorative rings simultaneously.

evidence.observation: The hero and planning photographs share a warm, tactile workshop treatment, but the Local Context image switches to a clean, lush modern residential street with a different visual character.
The contrast between sepia mechanical still life and polished residential streetscape remains conspicuous at full-page scale.
The workshop photographs feel materially connected, while the street image reads as a separate generic stock or rendered scene and does not clearly evoke Tucson.

imageryCoherence.fix: Use a contemporary Tucson commuter or neighborhood photograph graded to match the warm workshop images, with an intentional bicycle-related focal detail.

evidence.observation: The full-page footer visibly states “Built for the neighborhood,” a business-attributed predicate not supported verbatim or in full by one confirmed clause.
The same unsupported tagline appears in the footer; the Confirm Details disclaimer itself is appropriately restrained.
The unsupported tagline remains visible at the end of the page.

The claim policy authorizes only “Neighborhood bicycle repair for commuters” and “Walk in service is welcome”; it requires one clause to support the full predicate and prohibits assembling broader claims.

factualRestraint.fix: Replace “Built for the neighborhood” with verbatim confirmed wording or a non-attributed editorial label.

evidence.observation: Primary editorial headings are Canyon Wheelworks, Useful Directions, Local Context, and Confirm Details; all non-business headings contain no more than two meaningful words.
The same concise headings retain clean, controlled wraps.
Canyon Wheelworks uses the exact business-name exception, while all other primary headings remain at two words.

headlineDiscipline.fix: Preserve the concise two-word heading system.

evidence.observation: The initial viewport presents location, business name, explanation, action, and workshop image as one complete hero thought.
The hero remains a balanced text-and-image unit, with the action visible and no detached content.
The text block is complete before the photograph begins, and full-page evidence shows each lower section opening with its label, heading, and supporting beat together.

foldComposition.fix: Preserve the current section-opening order and keep headings attached to their explanatory copy.

evidence.observation: All three numbered planning rows are populated, and every image/text composition is complete without phantom columns.
The planning list, dark context composition, and final confirmation section retain complete counts and alignment.
All repeated rows stack consistently with numbers, titles, and descriptions; no empty slot or orphan card appears.

completeLayouts.fix: Preserve the three-row list and complete responsive stacking.

evidence.observation: Mechanical evidence reports 4 of 4 section first beats visible in normal, reduced-motion, and JavaScript-disabled states.
Mechanical evidence reports 4 of 4 section first beats visible in all three rendering states.
Mechanical evidence reports 4 of 4 section first beats visible in all three rendering states, and the screenshots show each lower section beginning with visible meaningful content.

firstBeatVisibility.fix: Preserve immediate rendering of every section's first beat.

evidence.observation: Hero and lower-section text sits on solid cream or charcoal fields rather than over the photographs; image-adjacent copy remains readable.
Text remains separated from all photographic crops, including the dark Local Context composition.
Photographs stack independently below or beside solid-background text, so difficult workshop and sunset crops do not reduce text contrast.

imageContrast.fix: Continue keeping informational text on solid color fields rather than over photography.

evidence.observation: Only one staged hero entrance is declared; the normal initial composition is readable, reduced motion disables it at 0.01ms, and the JavaScript-disabled target remains visible.
The single motion root passes normal, reduced-motion, and no-JavaScript contracts without delaying any first beat.
The same single entrance remains restrained, with all content visible under normal, reduced-motion, and JavaScript-disabled observations.

motionRestraint.fix: Keep motion limited to the single nonessential hero entrance.

evidence.observation: Workbench tools, bicycle wheel, repair environment, and desert route share warm amber grading and deliberate mechanical focal crops.
The same warm workshop and Tucson-route visual language remains consistent after responsive cropping.
Stacked photographs retain matching color treatment, subject matter, and contemporary editorial framing.

imageryCoherence.fix: Preserve the warm commissioned-shoot appearance and consistent crop treatment.

evidence.observation: Visible claims are limited to planning guidance, the confirmed commuter-repair clause, the confirmed walk-in clause, and explicit language that details are unconfirmed.
No unsupported prices, hours, address, transactional action, turnaround, or inferred repair service is presented.
The full page repeats the same restrained guidance and directs visitors only to confirm details with the business.
Neighborhood bicycle repair for commuters and Walk in service is welcome appear verbatim in confirmedUserClauses; the claim policy otherwise requires guidance-only language.

factualRestraint.fix: Continue using confirmed clauses verbatim and avoid adding inferred services or transactional language.
